_comment: Spaced repetition review schedule for the study-agent.
_theory: Based on the Leitner system and Ebbinghaus forgetting curve. Reviews at increasing intervals reinforce memory before it decays.
_intervals_note: Day +3:  First review. Item is fresh but not yet encoded in short-term memory.
Day +7:  Second review. Reinforces the short-to-medium term transfer.
Day +21: Third review. Tests whether the concept survived 3 weeks without use.
Day +60: Maintenance review. Keeps rarely-used knowledge accessible.
_failure_reset_note: On a failed review, reset to this index in the intervals array. 0 = back to first interval (3 days). Set to 1 to reset to 7-day interval instead.

priority_modifiers._comment: Multiply base intervals by these factors per priority level.
priority_modifiers._note: High-priority items keep the base schedule. Low-priority items are spaced further apart to focus limited review time on important concepts.

_max_daily_reviews_note: Cap the number of items presented per review session. Prevents overwhelm and maintains review quality.
_completion_note: After completing all intervals, items transition to this state. 'known' means the item is archived but not deleted.
_failed_state_note: Failed items remain in 'learning' state and are rescheduled.
